decks.notes: This deck focuses on locking down the opponent's options through a combination of artifacts and disruption spells, with an emphasis on controlling the board and preventing the opponent from executing their game plan effectively. Utilizing powerful artifacts like Planar Nexus, Trinisphere, and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to slow down and hinder the opponent's progress while setting up a win condition with cards like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. By establishing a prison-like environment on the battlefield, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and secure victory through superior resource management.

The main goal of this deck is to apply early pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan through tempo plays such as counter spells, removal, and hand disruption. It seeks to quickly establish a board presence with efficient creatures like Brazen Borrower and Murktide Regent while maintaining control with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and filtering, while Nethergoyf and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares offer additional threats. The deck's ability to tempo out opponents with cards like Daze and Wasteland makes it a powerful contender in the Legacy format.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources and controlling the board through a combination of land destruction, discard spells, and removal. It looks to grind out the opponent with cards like Smallpox and Wasteland, while also utilizing cards like Urza's Saga and Life from the Loam for value. The deck can quickly generate advantage with cards like Mox Diamond and Eumidian Hatchery, while also utilizing threats like Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis and Barrowgoyf to pressure the opponent. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and control the game until it can secure a win.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying threats like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler while disrupting the opponent with cards like Lightning Bolt and Force of Will. The deck aims to control the game tempo by leveraging cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to set up the perfect plays. With mana denial from Wasteland and efficient counter spells like Daze, the deck looks to maintain pressure on the opponent while protecting its own threats. Murktide Regent and Cori-Steel Cutter add additional power to the board, creating a fast and aggressive playstyle.

The deck focuses on utilizing disruptive spells and efficient creatures to control the game tempo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By combining powerful cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Hymn to Tourach with aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters, the deck aims to apply early pressure while maintaining control over the game. The mana base is designed to support the deck's multiple colors and maximize consistency. Overall, the deck's key strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while presenting a fast clock to close out the game.

The deck focuses on aggressively controlling the board by utilizing powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize. The inclusion of cards like Orcish Bowmasters and Goblin Bombardment adds additional threats and reach to close out games quickly. The mana base is optimized for consistent access to all colors needed, while also including land destruction with Wasteland. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of early pressure and disruption, maintaining tempo throughout the game.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly mill through your entire library and win the game with Thassa's Oracle or a combination of Undercity Informer and Bridge from Below. By casting spells like Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, and Simian Spirit Guide to generate mana, you can chain together a series of card-draw and graveyard recursion effects to achieve a fast and explosive win condition. The deck revolves around a all-in combo strategy, aiming to win the game before your opponent has a chance to react.

The main focus of this UWx Control Legacy deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive cards like Stifle and Wasteland. The deck aims to control the board and hand with cards like Quantum Riddler and Teferi, Time Raveler while generating card advantage with Ponder, Brainstorm, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck also utilizes powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to generate value and pressure the opponent. The mana base is optimized for consistency and flexibility to support the control game plan.

This deck focuses on quickly playing a high number of spells in order to win the game using a combo strategy. By playing powerful cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Simian Spirit Guide, the goal is to quickly fill the graveyard with key combo pieces like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer. By using cards like Reanimate and Dread Return, the deck is able to bring back combo pieces from the graveyard to quickly win the game by milling through the deck and winning with Thassa's Oracle. The deck utilizes Pact of Negation to protect its combo and disrupt opponent's plays.

The main concept of the deck is to quickly assemble a powerful combo using a mix of creatures, spells, and rituals to mill cards from the library and win the game with Thassa's Oracle or another win condition. The deck aims to use fast mana acceleration and disruption spells to execute the combo as quickly and efficiently as possible, allowing for a win as early as the first few turns of the game. The deck is highly focused on executing its game plan and disrupting the opponent's strategies.

The deck focuses on using cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Once Upon a Time to quickly fill up the graveyard and cheat out powerful creatures like Wight of the Reliquary and Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis. Utilizing cards like Elvish Reclaimer and Sylvan Safekeeper, the deck aims to ramp up mana quickly and protect its threats. With a mix of land destruction with cards like Wasteland and powerful combos like Boseiju, Who Endures and Moonshadow, the deck aims to overwhelm and outvalue opponents in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly play as many cards as possible to fill the graveyard with creatures, then use reanimation spells like Reanimate and Dread Return to bring back key creatures such as Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer. This allows the player to mill their entire library and win the game with Thassa's Oracle. The deck also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Pact of Negation to protect the combo and ensure a fast and efficient win. The deck focuses on explosive, all-in combo plays to quickly win the game.

The main concept of this deck is to lock down the opponent by using powerful artifacts to create a prison-like board state. By utilizing cards like Trinisphere, Candelabra of Tawnos, Karn, the Great Creator, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, the deck aims to control the game and deny the opponent resources. With powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Kozilek's Command, the deck can also close out the game quickly once the opponent's options are limited. The goal is to establish dominance through artifact control and overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and disrupting their game plan through counterspells and removal spells. It utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi to generate card advantage and board control. The presence of land destruction with cards like Wasteland adds another layer of disruption, while cards like Stifle and Force of Will help protect your game plan. The deck aims to grind out the opponent by out-valuing them with card advantage and strategic plays, ultimately gaining control of the game and securing victory.

The deck focuses on quickly milling through the library to put cards like Narcomoeba and Bridge from Below into the graveyard, while also using fast mana like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to power out spells that facilitate the combo. It aims to quickly assemble a winning combination of cards that allow the player to win the game without needing to cast any actual spells, relying instead on triggers and sacrificing creatures to secure victory in a single explosive turn.

This deck focuses on playing artifacts quickly and efficiently to generate mana and card advantage. Utilizing cards like Mystic Forge and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to create a powerful board state that can overwhelm the opponent. By combining cards like Grim Monolith, Manifold Key, and Urza's Tower, the deck can consistently produce large amounts of mana to play high-impact threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Soul-Guide Lantern and Kozilek's Command to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining its own game plan.
